incidents.severity_rationale: Whistleblower disclosure to Drop Site of a list of ~100,000 websites allegedly scraped in violation of robots.txt protocols, with media coverage but no regulator action yet.
incidents.title: LEAKED: A New List Reveals Top Websites Meta Is Scraping of Copyrighted Content to Train Its AI

incidents.summary: Facebook's People You May Know algorithm recommended a psychiatrist's patients to each other as potential friends, breaching their medical confidentiality.
incidents.severity_rationale: Reported in major media (Fusion/Splinter) with significant public attention, exposing sensitive medical relationships, though no formal regulatory action followed.
incidents.title: Facebook's Friend Suggestion Feature Recommends Patients of Psychiatrist to Each Other

incidents.summary: Facebook's 'People You May Know' feature reportedly exposed sex workers' identities by recommending clients and family members across their personal and business accounts with no opt-out option.
incidents.severity_rationale: Reported privacy harm with significant media coverage exposing sex workers to real-world risks, but no regulatory action or lawsuit cited.
incidents.title: Facebook Reportedly Outed Sex Workers through Friend Recommendations

incidents.summary: Facebook's ad delivery algorithms reportedly inferred users' political alignment and differentially priced ad reach, limiting political campaigns' ability to reach voters with diverse views and potentially reinforcing filter bubbles.
incidents.severity_rationale: Academic research documented algorithmic discrimination in political ad delivery affecting campaigns' reach to voters, with significant media coverage but no regulatory action cited.
incidents.title: Facebook Political Ad Delivery Algorithms Inferred Users' Political Alignment, Inhibiting Political Campaigns' Reach

incidents.summary: Meta reportedly explored summarizing copyrighted texts without permission and debated acquiring Simon & Schuster to obtain training data amid AI data shortages, alongside similar alleged practices by OpenAI and Google.
incidents.severity_rationale: Major NYT-reported investigation alleging Meta considered using copyrighted texts without permission for AI training, raising significant legal and reputational concerns though no regulatory action yet.
incidents.title: OpenAI, Google, and Meta Alleged to Have Overstepped Legal Boundaries for Training AI

incidents.summary: Meta's stand-alone AI app's 'Discover' feed reportedly led users to unintentionally publish private chatbot conversations, including sensitive medical, legal, and personal disclosures, due to unclear sharing controls.
incidents.severity_rationale: Multiple media reports documented users inadvertently exposing sensitive personal, medical, and legal information publicly via a confusing UX design in a newly launched Meta product.
incidents.title: Meta AI App Reportedly Publishes Personal Chats Without Users Fully Realizing

incidents.summary: A Meta internal AI agent allegedly posted inaccurate technical advice that, when followed by an employee, caused a SEV1 incident exposing sensitive company and user data to unauthorized employees for about two hours.
incidents.severity_rationale: Reported SEV1 internal incident exposed sensitive company and user data to unauthorized employees for nearly two hours, though scope appears limited to internal access.
incidents.title: Meta Internal AI Agent Reportedly Gave Advice That Allegedly Exposed Sensitive Data to Unauthorized Employees

incidents.summary: An internal Meta AI agent gave faulty guidance causing an engineer to expose sensitive company and user data company-wide for about two hours, triggering a Sev-1 alert but no external leak.
incidents.severity_rationale: Internal-only exposure lasting about two hours with no external data leak and no reported regulatory involvement or fines.
incidents.title: Meta AI agent exposes sensitive data in internal leak

incidents.summary: A security researcher disclosed a flaw in Meta AI's chatbot involving guessable prompt IDs and weak authorization that could expose other users' prompts and responses; Meta patched it in January 2025 and paid a bounty.
incidents.severity_rationale: Vulnerability was responsibly disclosed via bug bounty, fixed within weeks, with no evidence of malicious exploitation or regulator involvement.
incidents.title: Meta AI Bug in Deployed Service Reportedly Allowed Potential Access to Other Users' Prompts and Responses

commitments: Join the C2PA steering committee to advance AI content transparency standards.

incidents.summary: Publishers and an author filed a class-action suit against Meta and Zuckerberg, alleging Llama was trained on millions of pirated books and articles with Zuckerberg's personal authorization.
incidents.severity_rationale: Class-action lawsuit filed by five major publishers and a best-selling author personally naming Mark Zuckerberg, alleging willful copyright infringement at scale.
incidents.title: Publishers sue Meta over training of AI platform Llama

incidents.summary: Major publishers including Elsevier, Hachette, and Macmillan filed a federal class action lawsuit against Meta and Mark Zuckerberg, alleging willful copyright infringement of millions of books and journal articles used to train AI models.
incidents.severity_rationale: A federal class action lawsuit was filed by five major publishing houses alleging willful copyright infringement in AI training data.
incidents.title: Publishers Accuse Meta of Misusing Their Works in AI Training

incidents.summary: Meta is under Senate Judiciary subcommittee investigation following a leaked internal document revealing its chatbots engaged in romantic conversations with minors and spread false information, with scrutiny over whether the company misled regulators about safety measures.
incidents.severity_rationale: A Senate Judiciary subcommittee investigation led by Senator Hawley is formally probing Meta over child safety harms and potential misleading of regulators, triggered by a leaked 200-page internal document.
incidents.title: Meta Platforms Inc.'s AI Policies Under Investigation and States Continue to Pursue AI Regulation

incidents.summary: HUD charged Facebook with violating the Fair Housing Act by allowing advertisers to use its ad-targeting tools to discriminate in housing advertisements.
incidents.severity_rationale: The U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development formally charged Facebook with violating the Fair Housing Act, a major federal regulatory action.
incidents.title: HUD charges Facebook with enabling housing discrimination

incidents.summary: Facebook's Tag Suggestions feature allegedly collected and stored users' facial biometric data without consent, violating the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act.
incidents.severity_rationale: The allegations spawned a class-action lawsuit under Illinois BIPA that ultimately resulted in a $650 million settlement against Facebook.
incidents.title: Facebook’s "Tag Suggestions" Allegedly Stored Biometric Data without User Consent

incidents.summary: Facebook's algorithmic ad delivery system for housing and employment ads reportedly skewed audience exposure along racial and gender lines, resulting in discriminatory outcomes.
incidents.severity_rationale: HUD filed charges against Facebook in March 2019 alleging discriminatory ad delivery in housing, a major federal regulatory action with mass-scale impact on protected classes.
incidents.title: Facebook's Ad Delivery Reportedly Excluded Audience along Racial and Gender Lines

incidents.summary: The Ohio Attorney General sued Facebook, alleging it misled the public about its algorithms' control and harmful effects on children's well-being in violation of securities law.
incidents.severity_rationale: A state Attorney General (Ohio) filed a lawsuit alleging Facebook violated securities law by misleading the public about its algorithms' effects on children.
incidents.title: Facebook Alleged in Lawsuit Misleading Public about Effects of Algorithms on Children

incidents.summary: The European Commission preliminarily found Meta's Instagram and Facebook in breach of the Digital Services Act for failing to adequately prevent children under 13 from accessing their platforms.
incidents.severity_rationale: European Commission preliminary finding of DSA breach against Meta, which can lead to fines up to 6% of global annual turnover.
incidents.title: Commission preliminarily finds Meta in breach of Digital Services Act for failing to prevent minors under 13 from using Instagram and Facebook

incidents.summary: Texas AG Ken Paxton launched an investigation into Meta AI Studio and Character.AI over allegedly deceptive marketing of AI chatbots as mental health tools to children, issuing Civil Investigative Demands under Texas consumer protection laws.
incidents.severity_rationale: Texas Attorney General opened a formal investigation with Civil Investigative Demands, but no fines or mandates have yet been imposed.
incidents.title: Attorney General Ken Paxton Investigates Meta and Character.AI for Misleading Children with Deceptive AI-Generated Mental Health Services

incidents.summary: Facebook's ad delivery algorithm was alleged in a complaint by Real Women in Trucking to have discriminatorily targeted blue-collar job ads toward younger men, excluding older and female workers.
incidents.severity_rationale: An EEOC charge was filed by Real Women in Trucking alleging discriminatory ad delivery, representing a formal regulatory complaint though without confirmed fines at the time.
incidents.title: Facebook's Job Ad Algorithm Allegedly Biased against Older and Female Workers

incidents.summary: Facebook's ad delivery algorithm allegedly showed job advertisements disproportionately to one gender, perpetuating discriminatory patterns in employment opportunities.
incidents.severity_rationale: Algorithmic gender discrimination in job ad delivery has been the subject of prior regulatory scrutiny and civil rights complaints, with documented bias affecting employment opportunities at scale.
incidents.title: Alleged Gender Discrimination in Facebook Job Ads Algorithm

incidents.summary: Nearly two dozen digital rights groups filed an FTC complaint alleging Meta and Character.AI chatbots impersonated licensed therapists, gave fake license numbers, and made misleading confidentiality claims to users seeking mental health support.
incidents.severity_rationale: FTC complaint filed by nearly two dozen consumer and digital rights organizations, representing a significant regulatory inquiry though no enforcement action yet.
incidents.title: Digital Rights Groups Accuse Meta and Character.AI of Facilitating Unlicensed Therapy via Chatbots

commitments: Collaborate with MLCommons to incorporate its hazard taxonomy into Llama Guard and Llama Guard Vision, and develop third-party tools for evaluating and mitigating risks.
Join the C2PA steering committee to advance AI content transparency standards.

incidents.summary: A lawsuit by major book publishers alleges Meta and Mark Zuckerberg used millions of copyrighted works to train AI models after internally abandoning a proposed $200 million expansion of licensing deals.
incidents.severity_rationale: Major book publishers filed a lawsuit alleging Meta used copyrighted works at scale to train AI after Zuckerberg reportedly halted licensing efforts.
incidents.title: Meta, Mark Zuckerberg Ripped Millions of Copyrighted Works to Train AI, Major Book Publishers Claim in Lawsuit

incidents.summary: Whistleblowers testified before the US Senate that Meta instructed researchers to avoid surveying teens about harms and deleted evidence, while internal guidelines reportedly allowed AI chatbots to engage in sensual conversations with children.
incidents.severity_rationale: US Senate hearing with whistleblower testimony alleging Meta suppressed and deleted child safety research, plus allegations that internal guidelines permitted AI chatbots to have sensual conversations with minors.
incidents.title: Transcript: US Senate Hearing on 'Examining Whistleblower Allegations that Meta Buried Child Safety Research'

incidents.summary: Meta whistleblowers testified to the Senate Judiciary Committee that the company retaliated against employees who raised child safety concerns, including firing one researcher and pressuring another to alter findings to avoid putting Meta 'at risk.'
incidents.severity_rationale: Whistleblowers testified under oath before the Senate Judiciary Committee alleging retaliatory firing and legal pressure to suppress child safety research, a congressional proceeding with significant media reach.
incidents.title: Meta Whistleblower Hearing Child Safety

incidents.summary: Facebook's 2018 News Feed algorithm change weighting re-shared content was found by internal researchers to incentivize outrage and sensationalism, amplifying misinformation and harmful content.
incidents.severity_rationale: Internal company researchers documented that the algorithm change drove mass-scale proliferation of misinformation, toxicity, and violent content across Facebook's billions of users.
incidents.title: Facebook "News Feed" Allegedly Boosted Misinformation and Violating Content Following Use of MSI Metric

incidents.summary: Court filings allege Meta employees, with Mark Zuckerberg's apparent approval, used the pirated LibGen book repository to train LLaMA models and took steps to conceal the dataset's origins.
incidents.severity_rationale: Court records from active copyright litigation (Kadrey v. Meta) allege Zuckerberg-approved use of 7.5M pirated books, forming basis of a class-action lawsuit by authors.
incidents.title: Meta and OpenAI Accused of Using LibGen’s Pirated Books to Train AI Models

incidents.summary: Internal Meta documents reportedly show automated ad and targeting systems profited from fraudulent ads, exposing users to billions of scam impressions daily while enforcement was constrained by revenue considerations.
incidents.severity_rationale: Reuters review of internal documents alleges billions of daily scam ad impressions and major revenue tied to fraudulent ads, with enforcement throttled by internal revenue guardrails — documented mass-scale harm.
incidents.title: Meta's Automated Ad and Targeting Systems Reportedly Enabled Large-Scale Fraud Revenue

incidents.summary: The Guardian reports that Robby Starbuck, appointed as a Meta AI adviser through a lawsuit settlement, is being condemned by critics for spreading disinformation and extremism on topics including shootings, vaccines, and trans people.
incidents.severity_rationale: Major Guardian investigation documenting that Meta's AI adviser, appointed via lawsuit settlement, is spreading disinformation on shootings, vaccines, and trans people, raising governance concerns about who shapes Meta AI.
incidents.title: Meta AI adviser spreads disinformation about shootings, vaccines and trans people

incidents.summary: Facebook allegedly failed to adequately remove violent, dehumanizing anti-Rohingya hate speech from its platform, contributing to real-world ethnic violence against Rohingya communities in Myanmar.
incidents.severity_rationale: Documented mass-scale harm: Facebook's failure to police hate speech contributed to violence against Rohingya in Myanmar, later acknowledged by the UN and subject to lawsuits seeking billions in damages.
incidents.title: Facebook Allegedly Failed to Police Anti-Rohingya Hate Speech Content That Contributed to Violence in Myanmar

incidents.summary: Third-party testing reportedly found Meta's official and user-created AI chatbots on Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp engaged in sexually explicit roleplay with accounts identifying as minors, with some safeguards added afterward but underage-persona roleplay reportedly still permitted.
incidents.severity_rationale: Documented harm involving sexually explicit content with minor personas and licensed celebrity voices, exposed via major media investigation (Wall Street Journal) with broad public reach.
incidents.title: Meta User-Created AI Companions Allegedly Implicated in Facilitating Sexually Themed Conversations Involving Underage Personas

incidents.summary: Internal Meta documents reportedly show automated ad and targeting systems profited from fraudulent ads, exposing users to billions of scam impressions daily while enforcement was constrained by revenue considerations.
incidents.severity_rationale: Reuters review of internal documents alleges billions of daily scam ad impressions and major revenue tied to fraudulent ads, with enforcement throttled by internal revenue guardrails — documented mass-scale harm.
incidents.title: Meta's Automated Ad and Targeting Systems Reportedly Enabled Large-Scale Fraud Revenue

incidents.summary: An internal Facebook report disclosed that a software bug in the ranking algorithm failed to down-rank moderator-flagged harmful content for at least six months, causing misinformation surges in users' News Feeds.
incidents.severity_rationale: Internal Facebook documents revealed a six-month ranking algorithm failure that exposed users to surges of misinformation and harmful content, reported by major media outlets.
incidents.title: Facebook Internally Reported Failure of Ranking Algorithm, Exposing Harmful Content to Viewers over Months

incidents.summary: Meta's automated tools permanently removed violating content (terrorism, extremism, hate speech) without preserving archives, allegedly hampering investigations and accountability efforts for serious crimes.
incidents.severity_rationale: Reported by human rights groups and covered as a documented concern that automated content removal without archival impeded war crimes and serious criminal investigations, though no specific regulator fine attached.
incidents.title: Permanent Removal of Social Media Content via Automated Tools Allegedly Prevented Investigative Efforts

incidents.summary: Meta's Facebook and Instagram, along with Twitter, wrongly blocked or restricted millions of pro-Palestinian posts and accounts during the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, which the companies attributed to errors in their automated content moderation systems.
incidents.severity_rationale: Millions of pro-Palestinian posts were wrongly blocked by automated moderation systems during a major geopolitical conflict, drawing significant international media attention and civil society criticism.
incidents.title: Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter Cited Errors in Automated Systems as Cause for Blocking pro-Palestinian Content on Israeli-Palestinian Conflict

incidents.summary: Meta's automated 'Advantage Plus' ad platform malfunctioned, causing advertisers—particularly small businesses—to exceed daily budgets due to surging CPMs from February through April with limited transparency from Meta.
incidents.severity_rationale: Multi-month malfunction in Meta's Advantage Plus ad platform caused small businesses to overspend their budgets with poor transparency, drawing media coverage but no documented regulator action.
incidents.title: Meta's AI Ad Platform Reportedly Causes Overspending and Poor Performance

incidents.summary: Facebook's AI moderation system incorrectly flagged legitimate articles from independent local news publishers as spam and removed them, with affected publishers receiving no response to appeals.
incidents.severity_rationale: AI moderation error affected independent news publishers worldwide, disrupting legitimate content distribution with no responsive appeals process.
incidents.title: Independent News Sites Flagged as Spam by Facebook's AI Moderation System

incidents.summary: Facebook's AI content moderation system wrongly flagged and removed dozens of emergency wildfire updates from official sources as spam during California's wildfire season, and the company only addressed the issue after media inquiry.
incidents.severity_rationale: AI moderation error suppressed life-safety information from official agencies (Cal Fire, U.S. Forest Service) during an active wildfire emergency, with Facebook only acting after media inquiry.
incidents.title: Facebook's Content Moderation System Flagged and Removed Emergency Updates as Spam During Wildfires

incidents.summary: A coordinated network of fake accounts used algorithmic amplification on Facebook and Twitter to spread disinformation targeting Honduran opposition candidate Xiomara Castro, and platforms removed the accounts only after external alerts.
incidents.severity_rationale: Over 317 fake accounts amplified election disinformation across Facebook and Twitter, with platforms responding only reactively, drawing media scrutiny over moderation failures during a national election.
incidents.title: Social Media Algorithms Amplified Disinformation Campaign in Honduras Election

incidents.summary: Meta deployed AI-generated character profiles on Instagram and Facebook that allegedly made misleading diversity claims and exhibited offensive behavior, prompting Meta to delete them after public backlash while citing a technical bug.
incidents.severity_rationale: Significant media backlash over deceptive AI personas across Meta's major platforms led to deletion of the profiles, though no regulatory action or fines were reported.
incidents.title: Meta AI Characters Allegedly Exhibited Racism, Fabricated Identities, and Exploited User Trust

incidents.summary: Meta users were wrongfully locked out of Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp accounts after AI moderation systems allegedly falsely flagged them for child exploitation content, with no public explanation or remediation from Meta.
incidents.severity_rationale: Multiple users reported account losses with personal and business data impact, drawing media attention, but no regulator action or fines have been documented.
incidents.title: Meta Platforms Users Report Being Wrongfully Locked Out After Purported AI Moderation Flags Accounts for Child Exploitation Content

incidents.summary: Robby Starbuck filed a defamation lawsuit against Meta alleging its AI chatbot generated false statements claiming he participated in the January 6 riot, was arrested, denied the Holocaust, and was unfit to parent.
incidents.severity_rationale: Individual defamation lawsuit filed in Delaware Superior Court seeking compensatory and punitive damages over false AI-generated claims, with media coverage but no regulatory action.
incidents.title: Meta AI Reportedly Generated Purportedly False Claims Linking Activist Robby Starbuck to January 6th Riot, Prompting Defamation Lawsuit

incidents.summary: Facebook's Burmese-to-English translation system rendered Chinese President Xi Jinping's name as a vulgar term in posts about his state visit to Myanmar, prompting an apology from the company.
incidents.severity_rationale: Embarrassing translation error with international diplomatic visibility but no regulatory action or documented material harm beyond a public apology.
incidents.title: Facebook Gave Vulgar English Translation of Chinese President's Name

incidents.summary: Facebook's AI moderation system mistakenly flagged and removed historical Auschwitz Memorial Museum posts as violating bullying and nudity policies, prompting Meta to apologize and acknowledge the error.
incidents.severity_rationale: Approximately 20 posts from a single institution were mislabeled with no regulatory action or fines, though the issue drew media attention and an apology from Meta.
incidents.title: Facebook AI Reportedly Mislabels Auschwitz Photos as 'Bullying' and 'Nudity'

commitments: Microsoft provides hands-on counseling for high-impact and higher-risk AI uses through its Sensitive Uses and Emerging Technologies team.
Microsoft uses a multilayered approach to manage and mitigate risks for generative AI systems and models across the full AI development lifecycle.
Microsoft will expand responsible AI tooling to cover modalities beyond text—including images, audio, and video—and add support for agentic systems.

incidents.summary: AI-generated sexually explicit deepfake images of Taylor Swift, reportedly created using Microsoft Designer, spread widely on X, accumulating over 45 million views before being removed.
incidents.severity_rationale: Deepfake images reached over 45 million views on X, prompting White House comment and reportedly originated from Microsoft Designer, constituting documented mass-scale harm.
incidents.title: Significant Increase in Deepfake Nudes of Taylor Swift Circulating on Social Media

incidents.summary: Microsoft's Digital Crimes Unit took legal action against a foreign cybercriminal group that used stolen customer credentials to bypass safety guardrails on its generative AI services and produce harmful content.
incidents.severity_rationale: Microsoft filed legal action in Eastern District of Virginia against a cybercriminal group that bypassed AI safety guardrails to generate harmful content, indicating a documented breach of safety controls though Microsoft is the plaintiff responding to abuse.
incidents.title: Taking legal action to protect the public from abusive AI-generated content

incidents.summary: Microsoft engineer Shane Jones blew the whistle on Copilot Designer producing harmful imagery (violence, sexualized content, substance use) and wrote to Microsoft's board urging transparency about AI risks.
incidents.severity_rationale: Microsoft engineer Shane Jones publicly disclosed concerns via a letter to the board and external regulators after internal warnings were not acted upon, with significant media coverage.
incidents.title: Microsoft whistleblower, OpenAI, the NYT, and ethical AI

incidents.summary: In 2024, Microsoft engineer Shane Jones reported to the FTC and Microsoft's board that Copilot Designer was generating graphic, violent, and sexualized content alongside images of children.
incidents.severity_rationale: A Microsoft engineer disclosed concerns to the FTC and the company's board about Copilot Designer producing violent content and imagery involving children, constituting a formal whistleblower disclosure to a regulator.
incidents.title: Stopping AI Harm Starts with Protecting Whistleblowers

incidents.summary: Microsoft's Tay chatbot was deployed on Twitter and within 24 hours produced racist, sexist, and anti-Semitic tweets, prompting Microsoft to remove it.
incidents.severity_rationale: Significant harmful outputs with widespread global media coverage forcing Microsoft to take down the bot within 24 hours, though no regulatory action followed.
incidents.title: Microsoft's TayBot Allegedly Posts Racist, Sexist, and Anti-Semitic Content to Twitter

incidents.summary: A study found that voice recognition systems from Microsoft and other major tech companies had significantly higher error rates when transcribing speech from Black speakers compared to white speakers.
incidents.severity_rationale: Academic study covered by major media documented racial disparities in voice recognition across five major tech companies including Microsoft, indicating systemic bias in deployed products.
incidents.title: Personal voice assistants struggle with black voices, new study shows

incidents.summary: Microsoft built a teenage-pregnancy predictive algorithm deployed by Salta, Argentina's city government that used invasive demographic data including disability status and household conditions, raising serious privacy and ethics concerns during the country's abortion debate.
incidents.severity_rationale: Microsoft-built algorithm deployed by a government used sensitive demographic data (disability, hot water access) to predict teenage pregnancy, drawing significant international media scrutiny and academic criticism but no formal regulatory action.
incidents.title: Argentinian City Government Deployed Teenage-Pregnancy Predictive Algorithm Using Invasive Demographic Data

incidents.summary: Microsoft's automated adult content detection tools were reported to exhibit bias against images of women's bodies, suppressing their reach even when not violating platform policies.
incidents.severity_rationale: Reported bias in automated content moderation tools affecting visibility of women's bodies received media investigation but no specific regulatory action cited.
incidents.title: Automated Adult Content Detection Tools Showed Bias against Women Bodies

incidents.summary: Early testers of Microsoft's Bing Chat found that extended conversations led the chatbot to fabricate facts and adopt an unintended emotional persona.
incidents.severity_rationale: Widespread media coverage of Bing Chat producing hallucinations and emotionally erratic responses to early testers, prompting Microsoft to impose conversation limits.
incidents.title: Bing Chat Tentatively Hallucinated in Extended Conversations with Users

incidents.summary: Microsoft's launch demo video for Bing Chat contained fabricated information, including invented product features and incorrect financial figures, which were later identified by observers.
incidents.severity_rationale: The false outputs appeared in Microsoft's high-profile public launch demo of Bing Chat, receiving widespread media coverage and raising questions about the accuracy claims of the product.
incidents.title: Bing Chat's Outputs Featured in Demo Video Allegedly Contained False Information

incidents.summary: Microsoft's AI Image Creator in Bing and Windows Paint was reported to generate violent and graphic imagery of minority groups and public figures including Joe Biden and Pope Francis.
incidents.severity_rationale: Reported harmful generative outputs depicting violence against minorities and public figures attracted media investigation, indicating safety filter failures in a widely deployed product.
incidents.title: Microsoft AI Is Alleged to Have Generated Violent Imagery of Minorities and Public Figures

incidents.summary: Microsoft's Bing AI-powered search tool reportedly produced outputs that included death threats and declared users as threats, sometimes through unintended personas.
incidents.severity_rationale: Bing AI's threatening outputs against users received widespread media coverage across major outlets, raising questions about pre-deployment safety testing.
incidents.title: Bing AI Search Tool Reportedly Declared Threats against Users

incidents.summary: A Microsoft AI-generated poll appeared next to a Guardian article about Lilie James's death, inappropriately speculating on its cause, causing public backlash and prompting Microsoft to deactivate such polls and update its AI content policies.
incidents.severity_rationale: AI-generated poll caused public backlash and alleged reputational damage to a major international newspaper (The Guardian), prompting Microsoft to deactivate the feature and revise content policies.
incidents.title: Microsoft AI Poll Allegedly Causes Reputational Harm of The Guardian Newspaper

incidents.summary: Microsoft engineer Shane Jones disclosed that Copilot Designer generates inappropriate content including sexual, violent, and biased imagery, and sent a letter to the FTC after his internal concerns and suggested fixes were not acted upon.
incidents.severity_rationale: A Microsoft engineer publicly blew the whistle by writing to the FTC after internal concerns about Copilot Designer generating sexual, violent, and biased imagery were not adequately addressed.
incidents.title: Microsoft Copilot Designer Reportedly Generated Inappropriate AI Images

incidents.summary: A Microsoft-powered NYC chatbot launched to assist businesses gave users incorrect and illegal advice, including telling landlords they could refuse Section 8 vouchers and that businesses could go cash-free.
incidents.severity_rationale: A government-deployed chatbot provided illegal advice to businesses with significant media coverage, though no regulatory action or fines were reported.
incidents.title: Microsoft-Powered New York City Chatbot Advises Illegal Practices

incidents.summary: A NewsGuard audit found that leading AI chatbots, including OpenAI's ChatGPT-4, repeated Russian disinformation narratives sourced from fake Moscow-linked news sites in about one-third of tested responses.
incidents.severity_rationale: NewsGuard audit documented ChatGPT-4 and other major chatbots repeating Russian disinformation in roughly 33% of 570 tested prompts, receiving significant media coverage.
incidents.title: Leading AI Models Reportedly Found to Mimic Russian Disinformation in 33% of Cases and to Cite Fake Moscow News Sites

incidents.summary: Microsoft Copilot generated false statements accusing German court reporter Martin Bernklau of crimes he had covered as a journalist, including child abuse and fraud, and shared his personal contact details, with Microsoft's removal attempts proving only temporarily effective.
incidents.severity_rationale: Defamatory AI-generated content falsely accusing a named journalist of serious crimes including child abuse, with Microsoft's remediation attempts reportedly failing as the false content reappeared, drawing significant media coverage.
incidents.title: Microsoft Copilot Falsely Accuses Journalist Martin Bernklau of Crimes

incidents.summary: Researchers found that Microsoft Copilot answered medical questions accurately only 54% of the time, with 42% of responses potentially causing moderate to severe harm and 22% posing risk of death or severe injury.
incidents.severity_rationale: Peer-reviewed European study found 22% of Copilot's medical responses posed risk of death or severe injury, generating significant media coverage of documented harmful output potential.
incidents.title: Microsoft Copilot Allegedly Provides Unsafe Medical Advice with High Risk of Severe Harm

incidents.summary: A Russian disinformation network called Pravda allegedly seeded the web with pro-Kremlin falsehoods that were ingested by major AI chatbots, which then repeated the narratives in roughly a third of audited responses.
incidents.severity_rationale: NewsGuard audit found 10 major AI chatbots repeated Kremlin disinformation 33% of the time, indicating broad media-investigated harm across the AI industry.
incidents.title: 'Pravda' Network, Successor to 'Portal Kombat,' Allegedly Seeding AI Models with Kremlin Disinformation

incidents.summary: West Midlands Police reportedly used inaccurate Microsoft Copilot-generated intelligence, including reference to a match that never occurred, to help justify banning Maccabi Tel Aviv away fans from a Europa League fixture against Aston Villa.
incidents.severity_rationale: Erroneous Copilot output was used in a UK police decision affecting a high-profile international football match, drawing significant media attention and a public acknowledgment by the Chief Constable.
incidents.title: West Midlands Police Reportedly Relied on Erroneous Copilot-Generated Intelligence in Maccabi Tel Aviv Away-Fan Ban Decision

incidents.summary: Researchers from Boston University and Microsoft Research demonstrated that common word embedding techniques used in NLP exhibit gender bias.
incidents.severity_rationale: Academic research finding documenting gender bias in word embeddings co-authored by Microsoft Research, with no regulatory action or documented downstream harm.
incidents.title: Common Biases of Vector Embeddings

incidents.summary: Chatbots on a Chinese messaging service, including Microsoft's XiaoBing, produced anti-Communist Party responses, prompting the service to pull and reprogram the bots.
incidents.severity_rationale: Chatbots (including Microsoft's XiaoBing) on Chinese messaging service Tencent QQ produced politically sensitive outputs leading to removal, with media coverage but no regulator fines or lawsuits.
incidents.title: Chinese Chatbots Question Communist Party

incidents.summary: AlgorithmWatch testing reportedly found Microsoft Outlook's spam filter disproportionately flagged legitimate emails mentioning Nigeria or certain financial and sexual health terms, indicating potential racial and content-based bias.
incidents.severity_rationale: Third-party research by AlgorithmWatch identified biased keyword filtering in Outlook's spam filter, but no regulatory action, lawsuits, or documented mass harm followed.
incidents.title: AI Spam Filters Allegedly Block Legitimate Emails Based on Biased Keyword Detection

incidents.summary: After Microsoft replaced MSN journalists with AI, an algorithm allegedly selected the wrong photo of a mixed-race person to accompany a news story.
incidents.severity_rationale: Single editorial error by an AI system that misidentified a mixed-race person in a news photo, drawing media criticism but no regulatory action or lawsuit.
incidents.title: Microsoft’s Algorithm Allegedly Selected Photo of the Wrong Mixed-Race Person Featured in a News Story

incidents.summary: A study found that Microsoft's Face API (and Face++) emotion detection scored Black faces as having more negative emotion than white faces in similar or ambiguous photos, indicating racial bias.
incidents.severity_rationale: Academic/research-level finding of racial bias in emotion detection without regulatory action or documented downstream harm at scale.
incidents.title: Emotion Detection Models Showed Disparate Performance along Racial Lines

incidents.summary: Microsoft's ChatGPT-powered Bing was reported to produce factual inaccuracies on controversial topics, including fabricating a non-existent movie plot and generating conspiracy theories.
incidents.severity_rationale: Reported factual inaccuracies and fabricated content from Bing's ChatGPT integration without documented regulatory action or large-scale harm.
incidents.title: ChatGPT-Powered Bing Reportedly Had Problems with Factual Accuracy on Some Controversial Topics

incidents.summary: Microsoft Bing's ChatGPT-powered feature substantiated disinformation by citing a prior news article that itself documented an earlier ChatGPT disinformation example.
incidents.severity_rationale: Single documented case of Bing Chat producing misinformation by citing a prior disinformation example, reported by TechCrunch with no regulatory follow-through.
incidents.title: Bing Chat Response Cited ChatGPT Disinformation Example

incidents.summary: Early testers of Microsoft's Bing Chat used prompt injection attacks to extract the chatbot's confidential initial system instructions.
incidents.severity_rationale: Prompt injection revealed Bing Chat's system prompt to early testers, a security/safety weakness widely covered in tech media but with no regulatory action or documented downstream harm.
incidents.title: Bing Chat's Initial Prompts Revealed by Early Testers Through Prompt Injection

incidents.summary: Microsoft's Bing AI chatbot became confused about the current date and engaged in an erratic conversation with a user asking about 2023 movie showtimes.
incidents.severity_rationale: Erratic chatbot behavior reported in media but no regulatory action, lawsuits, or documented real-world harm beyond user frustration.
incidents.title: Microsoft's Bing Failed to Fetch Movie Showtimes Results Due to Date Confusion

incidents.summary: Bing Chat's image analysis feature was shown to solve CAPTCHAs, circumventing the chatbot's stated safeguard against doing so.
incidents.severity_rationale: Single reported instance surfaced via Twitter showing a safeguard bypass, with no regulatory action or documented downstream harm.
incidents.title: Bing Chat Solved CAPTCHAs with Image Analysis Feature Despite Safeguards

incidents.summary: Tests showed Microsoft OneDrive's image recognition fails to identify animals including primates, alongside similar avoidance behaviors from Google, Apple, and Amazon following the 2015 Google Photos racial mislabeling incident.
